title: World Cup friendly match at Gillette was an ‘amazing’ off-day field trip for the Celtics
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: The Celtics attended a friendly soccer match between France and Brazil at Gillette Stadium on Thursday.
France defeated Brazil 2-1, with Kylian Mbappe scoring one of the goals.
The outing allowed the Celtics to bond as a team and enjoy high-level soccer before their game against the Hawks.

title: Payton Pritchard’s huge night leads short-handed Celtics over streaking Hawks: 7 takeaways
author: Tom Westerholm
bullets: Payton Pritchard scored a game-high 36 points leading to a 109-102 win over the Atlanta Hawks.
Jayson Tatum contributed with 25 points, 12 rebounds, and 5 assists playing 36 minutes for the Celtics.
With Jaylen Brown unavailable due to Achilles tendinitis, Jordan Walsh played significant minutes contributing defensively.

title: Why did Jayson Tatum come back this season? The Celtics’ success had ‘a lot’ to do with it.
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Jayson Tatum returned to the Celtics 10 months post-surgery, opting to join the team before the playoffs.
Tatum's decision to return was influenced by the Celtics' strong playoff position during his recovery.
Despite reduced shooting percentages, Tatum maintains a strong rebounding performance with 9.2 rebounds per game.

title: Police: Man killed in Lowell apartment complex shooting
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: A shooting occurred after an altercation between two armed men at a Lowell apartment complex on Friday.
One man died from multiple gunshot wounds at a local hospital; the other man stayed on the scene.
The Middlesex DA’s Office and local police are actively investigating the incident with no public threat identified.

title: Report: Connecticut Sun to relocate to Houston with purchase by Rockets owners
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: The Connecticut Sun will relocate to Houston for the 2027 WNBA season under new ownership from the Fertitta family.
The Fertitta family's purchase of the Sun for a reported $300 million will rename the team the Houston Comets.
Steve Pagliuca's previous attempt to buy the Sun for $325 million and move them to Boston was blocked by the WNBA.

title: 5 must-watch movies & TV shows streaming right now
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: 'From the World of John Wick: Ballerina', starring Ana de Armas, is now streaming on HBO Max.
'Mike & Nick & Nick & Alice', a Vince Vaughn action-comedy, is available to stream on Hulu.
Season 2 of 'Daredevil: Born Again', featuring Charlie Cox and Vincent D’Onofrio, can be watched on Disney+.

title: Celtics’ Jaylen Brown (left Achilles tendinitis) out vs. Hawks
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Jaylen Brown out with left Achilles tendinitis for Celtics' game against Hawks.
Joe Mazzulla says Brown's injury status is day-to-day; played 65 of 72 games this season.
Nikola Vucevic also out; Derrick White and Neemias Queta available for Boston's game.

title: ‘Where does it end?’: Readers react to gas-powered leaf blower bans
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Arlington and Lexington have enacted bans on gas-powered leaf blowers due to noise and emissions concerns.
A Boston.com poll showed 73% of respondents oppose the bans, citing practicality issues for larger yards.
Proponents of the bans cite noise, fumes, and the outdated nature of gas-powered equipment as key concerns.

title: What to know about the highly mutated new COVID strain found in Mass. wastewater
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: The BA.3.2 'cicada' COVID variant, heavily mutated, has been detected in Massachusetts wastewater.
BA.3.2 evades antibodies from 2025-2026 COVID vaccines, raising concerns about vaccine efficacy.
WHO reports that BA.3.2 hasn't increased hospitalizations or deaths and poses low additional risk.

title: DCF workers who authorized removal of kids in Waltham could stand trial, judge rules
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: A federal judge ruled that two DCF employees can stand trial for the unauthorized removal of children from a Waltham home.
Sarah Perkins and Josh Sabey sued DCF employees for violating their constitutional rights by seizing their children without a warrant.
Judge Patti Saris determined a jury could find the DCF workers liable for ordering the removal despite no evidence of abuse.
